LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION DBA STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED - GRANTED WITH THIRTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT DID NOT ALLEGE ANY FACTS SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANT ACTED WITH MALICE OR KNEW OF THAT THE TABLE WAS DAMAGED. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION BY DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION DBA STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AS TO PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF DEPOSITIONS AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS CONTINUED TO MAY-29-2013 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE PRO TEM: JEFFREY WOHL; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION DBA STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AS TO PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF DEPOSITIONS AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. THE PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED, IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS PERMITTED TO PROCEED WITH FIVE PMK DEPOSITIONS OF HIS CHOOSING PROVIDED THAT THE LOCATION OF THE DEPOSITIONS ARE SET IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP 2025.250. THIS ORDER IS ISSUED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO SEEK FUTURE RELIEF FROM THE PROTECTIVE ORDER TO TAKE ADDITIONAL "WOBBLY TABLE" PMK DEPOSITIONS UPON A SHOWING OF COMPELLING NEED BASED ON WHAT IS LEARNED IN THE FIRST FIVE DEPOSITIONS OR OTHER DISCOVERY OR FACT INVESTIGATION. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order And Monetary Sanctions As To Pltff'S Ntc Of Depositions And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar May-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dennis P. Howell, Esq. of Grunsky Law Firm, Tel. 831.722.2444, for defendant and moving party;
Scott Righthand, Esq. of the Law Offices of Scott Righthand, Tel. 415.544.0115, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION DBA STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S Motion For Protective Order And Monetary Sanctions As To Plaintiff's Notice Of Depositions And Request For Production Of Documents. The protective order is granted, in part. Plaintiff is permitted to proceed with five PMK depositions of his choosing provided that the location of the depositions are set in accordance with CCP 2025.250. This order is issued without prejudice to Plaintiff's right to seek future relief from the protective order to take additional "wobbly table" PMK depositions upon a showing of compelling need based on what is learned in the first five depositions or other discovery or fact investigation. Sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION DBA STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Dennis P. Howell of the Grunsky Law Firm (831.722.2444) for defendant and moving party; Scott Righthand of the Law Office of Scott Righthand (544.0115) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Denied as to the request for punitive damages and granted as to the allegations regarding defendant's remedial measures. Paragraph 9 sufficiently alleges that defendant acted willfully and with a conscious disregard for plaintiff's safety. The allegations regarding defendant's remedial measures are irrelevant within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure section 436 because they do not support the negligence/premises liability cause of action. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Jan-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dennis P. Howell, Esq., from Grunsky Law Firm, for defendant Starbucks Corporation
Scott Righthand, Esq., Law Office, 415-544-0115, for plaintiff John Priest

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified.
DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION DBA STARBUCKS COFFEE COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike IS Granted with thirty days leave to amend. The complaint did not allege any facts showing that the defendant acted with malice or knew of that the table was damaged. Plaintiff is granted leave, if he can do so in good faith, to allege ultimate facts showing malice, fraud or oppression by defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
